response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and distributed systems engineer with genuine strengths in software craftsmanship, testing culture, and high-throughput backend work, but he falls critically short of the role's core requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience and 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or compute platform work; his C++ exposure appears limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, with no indication of sustained, production-level C++ systems work. His primary languages throughout his career have been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has worked with distributed micro-services at Dunnhumby handling high request volumes and has used AWS CDK and Kubernetes at a surface level, there is no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform. The gap between his actual profile and the minimum qualifications for this specific role is too wide to bridge with an interview.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), showing genuine large-scale systems exposure
Strong testing and software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source testing tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) reflect solid engineering discipline
Broad technology range and knowledge-sharing history (workshops, talks, open-source projects) indicates mentoring aptitude and technical communication skills
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments + a thesis project) versus the 8-year minimum hard requirement — this alone is disqualifying
No evidence of technical leadership over a cloud or infrastructure platform; roles are consistently individual-contributor or small-team oriented, not platform strategy ownership

Cloud platform depth is shallow — AWS CDK usage at Shopify and Kubernetes mentions in a side project do not demonstrate production-level cloud compute architecture or the ability to lead platform strategy
